extension.valueMarkdown:
In Georgia, Harold established with a new PCP and a geriatric neurologist at Emory. His MMSE dropped from 22/30 (mild) to 17/30 (moderate) over 6 months. He was switched from donepezil to a donepezil-memantine combination. His diabetes and hypertension management continued with his new PCP. He had one ED visit for a mechanical fall (no fracture, CT head negative).

Margaret, as his authorized representative, uses a patient-facing app to access Harold's records across both the Florida and Georgia systems.

Four sites across two states (FL, GA). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on jurisdictions (FL vs GA), organizations (4 providers), periods (pre-move Florida records vs post-move Georgia records), and scopes (can filter to just cognitive assessments or just diabetes management).

dataAbsentReason.text: Not formally administered due to cognitive status

note.text: PHQ-2 not formally administered due to cognitive impairment. Collateral from daughter Margaret: positive for depressed mood (persistent sadness and withdrawal since wife's death June 2024), negative for suicidal ideation.

coding.display: Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

note.text: Superseded by diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus (Condition/enc-001-diabetes) on 2015-06-12.

code.text: MMSE 24/30 with deficits in recall and orientation

note.text: MMSE 24/30 with deficits in recall (0/3 objects at 5 minutes), orientation to date (lost 2 points), serial sevens (lost 1 point), and mildly impaired clock drawing. Suspected early neurodegenerative process. Reversible metabolic causes (TSH, B12) ruled out. Depression screen negative. Referred to neurology for comprehensive evaluation.

code.text: Backed car into mailbox while pulling out of driveway

note.text: Reported by daughter Margaret Washington-Clarke. Harold backed his car into a mailbox while pulling out of the driveway. Dr. Okafor recommended patient no longer safe for driving and advised voluntary license surrender via Florida DHSMV.

code.text: Unopened pill bottles found at home (donepezil, lisinopril, metformin, atorvastatin)

note.text: Daughter reported finding unopened pill bottles at home since wife Dorothy's death on 06/22/2024. Dorothy had been providing daily medication reminders. Loss of this supervision has resulted in apparent medication non-adherence across all prescribed medications.

code.text: Neighbor found patient wandering two blocks from home, disoriented and unable to state destination

note.text: Reported by daughter Margaret Washington-Clarke. In early September 2024, a neighbor found Harold wandering two blocks from home, confused about where he was going. Consistent with worsening Alzheimer's disease following wife's death in June 2024.
Wife Dorothy deceased June 2024. Daughter reports persistent sadness and social withdrawal. Formal PHQ-2 deferred due to cognitive status.

reasonCode.text: New patient establishment of care; hypertension diagnosed

extension.valueMarkdown: Harold Washington, age 68, presented to Dr. Cynthia Okafor at Riverside Internal Medicine as a new patient after his previous PCP retired.

reasonCode.text: Laboratory results review, diabetes and hyperlipidemia diagnosed

extension.valueMarkdown: Dr. Okafor called Harold to discuss laboratory results drawn on 2015-06-05.
Harold presented for a lab-only visit. No vitals taken.
Harold, age 71, presented for his annual wellness exam. He reported feeling well, walking regularly, and attending church and social activities.

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness exam, Shingrix vaccine dose 1 administration

extension.valueMarkdown: Harold, age 73, presented for his annual wellness exam. He reports feeling well overall.

reasonCode.text: Annual wellness exam; cognitive concerns identified by spouse

extension.valueMarkdown: Harold, age 75, presented for his annual wellness exam. Dorothy accompanied him and expressed concern about Harold's memory.

reasonCode.text: Daughter reports wandering and driving incidents

extension.valueMarkdown: Margaret Washington-Clarke (daughter, authorized representative) called Dr. Okafor's office to report two concerning incidents.
Harold, age 78, presented for what will be his final visit at Riverside Internal Medicine before relocating to Decatur, Georgia to live with his daughter Margaret. Margaret was present for the visit.

Referral to Dr. Raymond Patel at First Coast Neurology Associates for comprehensive cognitive evaluation including brain imaging and neuropsychological testing

note.text: Referral to Dr. Raymond Patel at First Coast Neurology Associates (836 Prudential Drive, Suite 1400, Jacksonville, FL 32207) for comprehensive cognitive evaluation including brain imaging and neuropsychological testing.
Dose 1 of 2. Dose 2 due in 2 to 6 months. Patient counseled on common side effects including injection site soreness, fatigue, and myalgia.
